     The Numbers Business Report

Redeeming Love (2022)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $9,214,020Details
International Box Office $45,299
Worldwide Box Office $9,259,319
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $509,274
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $391,707
Total $900,981
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A retelling of the biblical book of Hosea set against the backdrop of the California Gold Rush of 1850. Angel expects only pain from those around her. Sold into prostitution as a child, Angel survives with hatred towards herself and the men that use her. She meets Michael Hosea, a farmer who believes God wants Angel to be his wife. Dire circumstances force Angel to accept his proposal, but when Michael defies her bitter expectations, her frozen heart begins to thaw. As Angel encounters a love unlike anything she ever experienced, feelings of unworthiness and shame cause her to run from a life she doesn’t think she deserves. As Michael sets out to find her, Angel discovers there is no brokenness that love can’t heal.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$3,530,180 (38.3% of total gross)
Legs:2.61 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:99.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:1,903 opening theaters/1,963 max. theaters, 3.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $10,465,311

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: January 21st, 2022 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: March 23rd, 2022 (Wide), released as Redeeming Love (리디밍 러브) (South Korea)
September 16th, 2022 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
September 17th, 2022 (Limited) (United Kingdom)
Video Release: February 8th, 2022 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for mature thematic content, sexual content, partial nudity, and strong violent content.
(Rating bulletin 2696 (cert #53388), 9/1/2921)
Running Time: 134 minutes
Geographical setting: California
Plot point: Romance
Social setting: Gold Rush
Source material: Modern Adaptation
Subgenre: Faith-Based Drama
Target audience: Faith-Based Film
Time period setting: 1850s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Mission Pictures International, Nthibah Pictures, Pure Flix, Lightworkers Media
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A retelling of the biblical book of Hosea set against the backdrop of the California Gold Rush of 1850. Angel expects only pain from those around her. Sold into prostitution as a child, Angel survives with hatred towards herself and the men that use her. She meets Michael Hosea, a farmer who believes God wants Angel to be his wife. Dire circumstances force Angel to accept his proposal, but when Michael defies her bitter expectations, her frozen heart begins to thaw. As Angel encounters a love unlike anything she ever experienced, feelings of unworthiness and shame cause her to run from a life she doesn’t think she deserves. As Michael sets out to find her, Angel discovers there is no brokenness that love can’t heal.
